title: ulmus macrocarpa f. macrophylla
intro: Ulmus macrocarpa f. macrophylla, also known as the Rock Elm tree, is a deciduous tree species native to North America, characterized by its broad, vase-shaped crown and large, elliptical leaves. The bark and leaves of the Rock Elm tree have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The health benefits of Ulmus macrocarpa f. macrophylla include its ability to support the immune system, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb in natural medicine. Herbal preparations of the Rock Elm tree typically involve the bark being dried and ground into a powder, which can be used in teas, tinctures, and capsules to harness its medicinal properties.
